string 2:
Excellent! That went smoothly. We'll have to hurry, though.
Multiple Digimon are being imprisoned somewhere in the lab.

I suggest we head for the security room.
There's bound to be some useful information there.
Wait a minute. Something's not right. Is that...
...a Digimon? And there's no sign of any personnel.

I have a bad feeling about this... But all we can do
right now is hurry on to the security room.

There doesn't seem to be any personnel around. 
That seems careless, but... it's good news for us.

I was afraid of this! It's Section B. The Digimon are being
held at the far end of the lab.
We'll have to break through security to get there.

Things may have gone smoothly at the lab entrance, but
access to Section B is restricted, even to most personnel.
My fake ID probably won't be good enough to get us in.

I'll have to make some updates to it. Let's head for the
server room.
The message on the screen reads, "Register a new ID?"

"...Final verification is necessary. Please enter the ID
number of an appropriate supervisor."

Uh-oh, there it is. I obviously don't have a supervisor's
ID number on me. We'll need to find one.

These researchers are every bit as lazy and disorganized
as I am. Search the rooms, I bet you'll find something.

You work on getting ahold of a supervisor's ID. There's
something else I want to look for in the meantime.
We can't leave Dr. Simmons here all by herself.
I'll stay behind with her.

But I'll be monitoring your situation the whole time.
Just give me a call if anything goes wrong.
Okay, sounds like we have a plan. You keep searching the floor.

There's a message on the screen.
"How to Use an Emergency ID → Whiteboard Memo"

I've issued you a new ID, but this is getting ridiculous.
How many times have I had to reissue one for you now?

You may be an excellent researcher, but you're completely
hopeless in every other way.

I stuck a note to the whiteboard to keep you from losing
this one.

From now on, don't bother with the ID. Just check the
whiteboard.
—Management

Sounds like there's a note stuck to a whiteboard somewhere
that might be of use. But there are a lot of whiteboards...

You'll have to go through them one at a time to see which
of them has that note.
There's a note.

"Stop leaving security information out where anyone can see
it."

"I've placed the critical documentation into a file.
It will be kept there going forward."

Guess they realized keeping it out in the open was not a good idea.
That note must be hidden somewhere in all of this paperwork.
You'll just have to dig through all of it...
There's a note.

"...New IDs cannot be issued during an emergency. A recovery ID
is stored in a storage device attached to the Admin Digimon..."

After thinking long and hard about the safest place to put
it, I stuck it to the back of the strongest Digimon here.

Warning! Warning! Intruder detected! Security systems
now on high alert!

Sounds like security just got a lot tighter! We'll have to
be more careful than ever.

I wonder what Digimon would be the admin? Guess we'll
just have to search.

I heard everything from Asuna. I'll issue the ID now,
so give me a minute.

But their sloppiness has proven to be our salvation.
Now, I'm going to issue the new ID. Give me a minute.

Unusual activity detected in the server room.
Security, please proceed to the server room immediately.

Well, that's not good... Enemies are headed this way,
but we can't get into Section B without that ID.
Asuna, Agent! Buy me some time!
...Not quite done yet!
Almost got it...!

Phew, that takes care of the ID. Thanks for giving me
enough time.
Now, let's head to Section B at the far end of the lab.
"Verifying ID... Authorization complete."

Okay, that seems to have worked.
Here. You hold onto the ID.

"I have compiled the results of all the reports on the
Digimon to be incorporated into the security system."

"Please look over everything and if you feel anything is
missing, let me know at the next meeting."

"Tonight's the big night! We'll be wining and dining five
doctors from Higashi-Shinjuku Surgical Hospital."

"While of course we'll have to allow for circumstances,
I want everyone to basically stick to Plan B."

"This is just a reminder about the next weekly meeting.
It will be held on Thursday at 3:00 PM, as usual."

An e-mail is displayed on the screen: "We are not
authorized to reissue supervisor IDs."

"Please contact Information Security. The General Affairs
Department is not your personal assistant. Thank you."

The computer belonging to the person who made that request
for a supervisor ID has to be here somewhere.
There's a note: "Regarding the Storage of Important Documents."

"Please store documents in accordance with company policy.
General Affairs is not responsible for lost documents."
An e-mail is displayed on the screen...

"As we always tell you, General Affairs is not your
personal assistant. We do not take orders for midnight snacks."
There's a sticky note on this computer...
"This computer is under repair."

"Please do not modify company property at your own discretion.
Further modifications will result in deductions from your pay."

"Sadly, the mixer with those surgeons from Higashi-Shinjuku
came to a rather disappointing conclusion."

"However, I hope to use this as a learning experience. I'm
already planning another get-together for next month."
There's a note.

"Ebemon have recently been added to the security system.
They are extremely reliable Digimon."

"Any questions regarding Ebemon should be directed to
Researcher Y in Group G."
There's a note.

"Shoyu Negi Ramen... 4"
"Corn Butter Miso Ramen... 2"

"Veggie Shio Ramen... 3"
"Gyoza... 4"
There's a note.

"If any unusual activity is detected in the facility, an alarm
will sound."

"The security system will activate at that time, so please
take shelter somewhere safe."
There's a note.

"Missed Delivery Notice:
Frozen articles could not be left in post box."

"Please contact us about redelivery.
Item Name: Mega Sweets Lucky Bag"
There's a note.

"Conference room reservations and vacation requests
have been migrated to the employees-only app."

"Please log in with your ID to perform these procedures.
Note: You cannot log in with a supervisor ID."

There were no fancy apps when I was there. It was really
inconvenient back then... HQ was prehistoric.

Well, no use complaining about it now. Let's look for clues
about that supervisor ID.
There's a note.
"Vocalist Recruiting Band Members!"

"Accepting auditions for all other parts. Let's shoot for
stardom with researcher rock!"
A document has been left here: "Data on Vulcanusmon."

"Appears interested in human subcultures. Easy to
communicate with."

"Of particular interest for its ability to create weapons on
the spot."

They must've gotten this information by force... Absolutely
disgusting...!

Sorry. My temper got the better of me, there. Let's look at
the other documents.

HiAndromon has been added to the security team for improved
performance.

He is currently the most powerful Digimon in the facility.
He is also reliable enough to be given a leadership role.
"Weekly Meeting Agenda"

"Progress on quarterly goals, security enhancements,
and measures against takeaway container litter."

"Reference Documents: Progress reports, security plans,
and list of restaurants which use recyclable containers."
"Precautions Regarding Security Digimon"

"Datamon are an extremely reliable addition to the
security team."

"However, they have a tendency to disrupt functional computers.
Please do not let Datamon get near any computers."
You did it! That's got the supervisor's ID number on it!

That turned into quite the runaround, didn't it?
Hurry on back.
That was a formidable opponent.

But now we've got that supervisor's ID number!
Please hurry back.

Wouldn't want to get spotted by the researchers.
Let's not do anything rash.
We don't need to enter that room. Let's not waste any time.

Vulcanusmon is being held in there, but I need to overwrite
this keycard ID before we can open the door.
To open this door, the keycard has to be overwritten.

I'll be monitoring your situation the whole time. Just give
me a call if anything goes wrong.

I'll stay here, too. You know... this reminds me of the
Shinjuku Underground eight years ago.

You go look for a supervisor's ID number. I've got my eye
out looking for something else.

Taking the former BlackGatomon as a model case, we've
managed to interact amicably with a few Digimon.

Unfortunately, the situation has since changed. I suggest heading
for the security room. There's bound to be some useful info there.

"I am not as idealistic as my predecessor. We must face these
Digimon with a strong will. Our survival depends on it."

It's like unleashing an untamable beast inside a deserted zoo. 
There's not a shred of beauty in it.

In any case, security might show up here. Since it'll take
a while to issue an ID, I want you to buy us some time.

A document is displayed on the screen: "My Predecessor's
Study on Social Unrest and Anomalous Phenomena."

"...Okay, okay. So humanity has overcome countless crises to date.
Seems to me that this miracle is the greatest anomaly of all."

"Why has increased social unrest led to more frequent
Digimon sightings and anomalous phenomena?"

"Considering that the outcome has been humanity's
continued survival, one hypothesis comes to mind."

"Like our immune system, helping to maintain homeostasis...
Digimon may be part of a reaction to crises in the world."
There is a memo that reads, "A Study of Digimon and Mythology."

"Humanity interprets natural disasters as the wrath of God, 
but in mythology, the presence of Digimon can be seen."

"Thus, are Digimon our damnation, wreaking destruction and chaos?
Or, conversely, are they our deliverance in times of calamity?"
A scribbled comment reads, "Response to My Predecessor."

"If Digimon are part of the world's immune response to crisis,
then why have these hostile Digimon come to target humans?"

"Isn't it because the developed human race is perceived as an abnormality 
that should be exterminated? Indeed, Captain Kuremi was almost killed."
We should be able to get out through there.
Leave this to me; I know this place as well as anyone.
I made a fake ID. This should let us get through the entrance.

The giant weapon hasn't been activated yet... 
Seems like we've gone back in time.

When was the last time you came through here? It feels like
ages ago.
A giant humanoid weapon. It hasn't been activated yet.
